identity.location: 972 College St, Toronto, ON M6H 1A5, Canada

narrative.summary: You're on a residential stretch of College West, past the Portuguese bakeries and vintage shops, stepping into a long and narrow dining room with warm wood and cream walls that catch the light just right. The counter seats are where you want to be—close enough to watch chefs work the line, plating each dish with the kind of precision usually reserved for much stuffier rooms. The cacio e pepe has the kind of emulsified, glossy sauce that proves they know what they're doing with pasta water and Pecorino, and you'll spend $70-90 per person with a bottle of wine.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You'll find this Italian spot on a residential stretch of College West, past the Portuguese bakeries and vintage shops. The counter seats are where you want to be—close enough to watch chefs work the line, plating each dish with the kind of precision usually reserved for much stuffier rooms. The dining room runs long and narrow, all warm wood and cream walls that catch the light just right.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The kitchen turns out refined Italian that doesn't try too hard—think grilled octopus with proper char and handmade ravioli that actually tastes like someone's nonna made it, if she had restaurant training. Their cacio e pepe has the kind of emulsified, glossy sauce that proves they know what they're doing with pasta water and Pecorino. Expect to spend around $70-90 per person with a bottle of wine.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Went here to celebrate my birthday and Giulietta was a pleasant experience and served amazing food.

Had a surprisingly very nice experience sitting at the counter. We originally wanted to sit in standard seating but booked counter because that was all that was available, but ended up being a great choice. Food was amazing, we got the grilled octopus, ravioli,  and cacio e pep. To top it off we got to see the amazing chefs cook and plate all the dishes.

Service was excellent, we felt very well taken care of and welcomed. Definitely looking forward to coming back and trying their other dishes."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Beef tartare melts in your mouth. It is prepared in a way that is easy to pick up and eat and the truffle flavour is wonderful.

Hokkaido scallop crudo is a fresh dish with fresh ingredients but flavour was lacking comparatively.

La Valentia - Neapolitan crust is excellent and chewy. The sausage packs a punch in terms of heat and salt. It goes well is the tomato sauce and roasted garlic.

Seafood pasta is cooked to al dente. It’s light and full seafood and juicy tomatoes.

Romanesco cauliflower is so unique; it’s green and chopped into tiny florets and roasted with crunchy almond pieces which makes it delightful.

Torta della nonna is like a light butter tart except with honey, pine nuts, and mascarpone cream. I highly recommend it. Tiramisu was nice (and big) but it was no contest against the torta della nonna."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We celebrated my wife’s birthday at Giulietta and had an amazing night. The space is cozy but lively, the service was excellent, and everyone was friendly. We reserved bar seats since no tables were available and ended up loving it. Watching the cocktails being made really added to the atmosphere. We ordered the beef tartare, fried olive and sausage ascolana, roasted squash with brown butter and sage, and of course the cacio e pepe. We also added the pan roasted pork chop, which was honestly one of the best things we’ve ever eaten. The photos don’t do this place justice. Everything was delicious and completely worth the hype. This is easily our new favourite Italian spot in the city. Thanks to the team at Giulietta for a wonderful night."
